name: Moralltach Sword
description: Moralltach, meaning "Great Fury" or "Great Anger", was a powerful and legendary sword in Irish mythology. It was originally owned by the hero Cúchulainn. The sword was said to be incredibly sharp and deadly, capable of cleaving through enemies with ease. Some accounts claim it was forged by the gods themselves, imbuing it with extraordinary power. Moralltach was more than just a weapon; it was a symbol of Cúchulainn's unmatched skill and ferocity in battle. It was often mentioned in conjunction with his spear, the Gáe Bulg, representing the pinnacle of his martial prowess. The sword was feared by his enemies, as its reputation preceded its use. It was believed to never strike a false blow, always finding its mark. Its use often turned the tide of battles in favor of Ulster. After Cúchulainn's death, the fate of Moralltach is somewhat unclear in different versions of the tales, with some accounts stating it was lost in battle, while others suggest it was taken as a trophy. Regardless, Moralltach remained a potent symbol of heroism and destructive power in Irish lore.
subtitle: The Sword of Great Fury
material: Unknown (possibly celestial iron or enchanted steel)
origin: Varied accounts; some say forged by gods, others obtained through heroic deeds.
currentLocation: Unknown; lost to time and legend.

keyMyths.title: Cúchulainn's Battles
keyMyths.description: Moralltach was instrumental in many of Cúchulainn's victories, cutting down countless foes. Its sharpness and accuracy were legendary on the battlefield.

keyMyths.title: Acquisition of the Sword
keyMyths.description: The exact method Cúchulainn acquired Moralltach varies, but it always involves a display of exceptional skill or courage. Some stories suggest it was a gift from a deity.

sources.author: Thomas Kinsella
sources.description: Translation of the Táin Bó Cúailnge, featuring Cúchulainn's battles.
sources.text: Translation of the Táin Bó Cúailnge, featuring Cúchulainn's battles.
